odeNavStructure.pageName: THE TREASURE OF COMMUNICATION: DISCOVERING ENGLISH THROUGH REAL-LIFE SITUATIONS

odeNavStructureProperty.value: THE TREASURE OF COMMUNICATION: DISCOVERING ENGLISH THROUGH REAL-LIFE SITUATIONS
THE TREASURE OF COMMUNICATION: DISCOVERING ENGLISH THROUGH REAL-LIFE SITUATIONS

odeComponent.htmlView:
Good morning everyone!

Think about this for a moment:
How many times do you communicate every day?
You send messages, ask for help, order food, watch videos, talk to friends, use social media… Communication is everywhere. But what happens when you need to do all that in another language?

Today, we are starting a new project called “The Treasure of Communication: Discovering English through Real-Life Situations.”
In this unit, English will not just be a school subject. It will become a real tool for communication. You will learn how to use English in everyday situations that could happen in real life: travelling, asking for information, solving problems, giving opinions, or interacting with other people.

The main goal is not to be perfect, but to communicate effectively, confidently and creatively. Throughout the project, you will work in teams, create dialogues and digital products, and practise speaking English in realistic contexts. You will also reflect on cultural diversity, cooperation and respectful communication.

At the end of the project, you will create your own final communicative task using digital tools such as Canva and Flip, where you will show how English can help us connect with people and situations in the real world.

So, are you ready to discover the treasure of communication?
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036CWOCRF","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Good morning everyone!

Think about this for a moment:
How many times do you communicate every day?
You send messages, ask for help, order food, watch videos, talk to friends, use social media… Communication is everywhere. But what happens when you need to do all that in another language?

Today, we are starting a new project called “The Treasure of Communication: Discovering English through Real-Life Situations.”
In this unit, English will not just be a school subject. It will become a real tool for communication. You will learn how to use English in everyday situations that could happen in real life: travelling, asking for information, solving problems, giving opinions, or interacting with other people.

The main goal is not to be perfect, but to communicate effectively, confidently and creatively. Throughout the project, you will work in teams, create dialogues and digital products, and practise speaking English in realistic contexts. You will also reflect on cultural diversity, cooperation and respectful communication.

At the end of the project, you will create your own final communicative task using digital tools such as Canva and Flip, where you will show how English can help us connect with people and situations in the real world.

So, are you ready to discover the treasure of communication?

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Communication is the process of exchanging information, ideas, feelings or opinions with other people. In English, communication is not only about grammar and vocabulary; it is also about understanding others and expressing ourselves clearly and appropriately in different situations.

There are different types of communication:

  • Oral communication: speaking and listening.
  • Written communication: messages, emails, texts or notes.
  • Non-verbal communication: gestures, facial expressions, body language and tone of voice.

In real-life situations, communication usually has a specific purpose, such as: asking for information,
giving opinions, solving problems, making requests, apologising, or interacting socially.

To communicate effectively in English, we need:

  • appropriate vocabulary,
  • correct grammatical structures,
  • clear pronunciation,
  • active listening,
  • and respectful interaction.

Communication also depends on the context and the register:

  • Formal language is used in professional or academic situations.
  • Informal language is used with friends or family.

Useful communication strategies include: asking for clarification, repeating information, using gestures,
paraphrasing, and cooperating with others during interaction.

English is an international language that helps people from different cultures communicate. For this reason, learning English also means developing respect for cultural diversity and improving intercultural understanding.

In this unit, students will practise English through realistic communicative situations to develop confidence, fluency and practical language skills for everyday life.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036NTGZOC","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Communication is the process of exchanging information, ideas, feelings or opinions with other people. In English, communication is not only about grammar and vocabulary; it is also about understanding others and expressing ourselves clearly and appropriately in different situations.

There are different types of communication:

  • Oral communication: speaking and listening.
  • Written communication: messages, emails, texts or notes.
  • Non-verbal communication: gestures, facial expressions, body language and tone of voice.

In real-life situations, communication usually has a specific purpose, such as: asking for information,
giving opinions, solving problems, making requests, apologising, or interacting socially.

To communicate effectively in English, we need:

  • appropriate vocabulary,
  • correct grammatical structures,
  • clear pronunciation,
  • active listening,
  • and respectful interaction.

Communication also depends on the context and the register:

  • Formal language is used in professional or academic situations.
  • Informal language is used with friends or family.

Useful communication strategies include: asking for clarification, repeating information, using gestures,
paraphrasing, and cooperating with others during interaction.

English is an international language that helps people from different cultures communicate. For this reason, learning English also means developing respect for cultural diversity and improving intercultural understanding.

In this unit, students will practise English through realistic communicative situations to develop confidence, fluency and practical language skills for everyday life.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Communication and tourism are closely connected because tourism depends on interaction between people from different countries, languages and cultures. In real-life situations, English is often used as an international language that allows tourists and local people to understand each other and communicate effectively.

In the tourism sector, communication is essential to give and receive information, ask for help, solve problems, offer services, explain cultural or historical aspects, and create positive social interactions.

Tourists use English in many everyday situations, for example: booking a hotel room, ordering food in a restaurant, asking for directions, visiting museums or monuments, shopping, or talking to tour guides.

At the same time, professionals working in tourism need communication skills to: welcome visitors,
provide clear instructions, answer questions politely, recommend places and activities, and help tourists feel comfortable and safe.

Good communication in tourism is not only about speaking correctly; it also involves active listening,
politeness, body language, cultural awareness, and the ability to adapt language to different people and situations.

Tourism also promotes intercultural communication because people learn about different traditions, lifestyles and perspectives. Through communication, tourists and local communities can exchange experiences and develop respect for cultural diversity.

For this reason, learning English through tourism-related situations helps students practise practical vocabulary and realistic conversations while developing confidence, cooperation and intercultural competence. It prepares them to communicate successfully in a globalised world where English is an important tool for real interaction.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036SAVAFP","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Communication and tourism are closely connected because tourism depends on interaction between people from different countries, languages and cultures. In real-life situations, English is often used as an international language that allows tourists and local people to understand each other and communicate effectively.

In the tourism sector, communication is essential to give and receive information, ask for help, solve problems, offer services, explain cultural or historical aspects, and create positive social interactions.

Tourists use English in many everyday situations, for example: booking a hotel room, ordering food in a restaurant, asking for directions, visiting museums or monuments, shopping, or talking to tour guides.

At the same time, professionals working in tourism need communication skills to: welcome visitors,
provide clear instructions, answer questions politely, recommend places and activities, and help tourists feel comfortable and safe.

Good communication in tourism is not only about speaking correctly; it also involves active listening,
politeness, body language, cultural awareness, and the ability to adapt language to different people and situations.

Tourism also promotes intercultural communication because people learn about different traditions, lifestyles and perspectives. Through communication, tourists and local communities can exchange experiences and develop respect for cultural diversity.

For this reason, learning English through tourism-related situations helps students practise practical vocabulary and realistic conversations while developing confidence, cooperation and intercultural competence. It prepares them to communicate successfully in a globalised world where English is an important tool for real interaction.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
¿Te atreves a practicarlo? Con esta aplicación de geogebra representarás sistemas y verás sus soluciones según sus coeficientes.

https://www.geogebra.org/m/vVgE488J (Ventana nueva)

Jos%E9%20Manuel%20Fern%E1ndez,https%3A//ggbm.at/5624156,Resoluci%F3n%20gr%E1fica%20de%20sistemas%20de%20dos%20ecuaciones%20lineales%20con%20dos%20inc%F3gnitas.,1,Autor%EDa
Actividad%20no%20completada,Actividad%20superada.%20Puntuaci%F3n%3A%20%25s,Actividad%20no%20superada.%20Puntuaci%F3n%3A%20%25s,Guardar%20la%20puntuaci%F3n

{"ideviceId":"20260509073036WXOYXW","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"","textTextarea":"

{"ideviceId":"20260509073036QVLDRM","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Here, we'll see how to organise the documents to work with your classmates:

  • Create a folder in your Drive account called "The Treasure of Communication: Discovering English through Real-Life Situations". It must include:
    • The different videos you have been using to create your own role-play.
    • All the elements you have been using for that.
  • Remember to share it with your teacher.

From here on, you will be responsible of your progress. Remember that your teacher will be assessing you, and the assessment criteria established for that are: 

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yIivdncMy3Dj7zQ8VKAJJHGmzIkdOrfNwdTTrQZGx74/edit?tab=t.0
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036CGZDNM","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Here, we'll see how to organise the documents to work with your classmates:

  • Create a folder in your Drive account called "The Treasure of Communication: Discovering English through Real-Life Situations". It must include:
    • The different videos you have been using to create your own role-play.
    • All the elements you have been using for that.
  • Remember to share it with your teacher.

From here on, you will be responsible of your progress. Remember that your teacher will be assessing you, and the assessment criteria established for that are: 

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yIivdncMy3Dj7zQ8VKAJJHGmzIkdOrfNwdTTrQZGx74/edit?tab=t.0 
","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
And now, let's create! Do you dare creating your own role-play?

Remember that it must be about a conversation between a tour guide and a tourist, focused on the contents of this unit. Here is an example of this:
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036RKLTBQ","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

And now, let's create! Do you dare creating your own role-play?

Remember that it must be about a conversation between a tour guide and a tourist, focused on the contents of this unit. Here is an example of this:

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

{"ideviceId":"20260509073036HGVFXL","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"","textTextarea":"","textFeedbackInput":"","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView: Just one more step to finish this task. Open Flip with your school account and record your final project.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036JJQPPD","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"
\n

Just one more step to finish this task. Open Flip with your school account and record your final project. 

\n
","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Tour Guide
Greeting tourists
Welcoming visitors
Giving information
Describing places
Giving directions
Recommending activities
Explaining rules or schedules
Answering questions

Tourist
Asking for information
Asking for clarification
Expressing opinions
Showing interest or surprise
Asking for recommendations
Asking for help
Thanking and responding politely

Explaining rules or schedules
Answering questions

Asking for clarification

Asking for help

odeComponent.htmlView:
Tour Guide Expressions
Welcome to…
Today we are visiting…
This place is famous for…
On your left/right, you can see…
I recommend visiting…
Please follow me.
The tour starts at…

Tourist Expressions
Excuse me, could you help me?
What is this place known for?
How old is this monument?
Could you repeat that, please?
Where can I buy tickets?
What do you recommend?
That sounds interesting!
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509073036UNIZPN","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Tour Guide Expressions
Welcome to…
Today we are visiting…
This place is famous for…
On your left/right, you can see…
I recommend visiting…
Please follow me.
The tour starts at…

\n

Tourist Expressions
Excuse me, could you help me?
What is this place known for?
How old is this monument?
Could you repeat that, please?
Where can I buy tickets?
What do you recommend?
That sounds interesting!

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

Tourism Vocabulary
monument
museum
sightseeing
landmark
tour
guide
visitor
brochure
map
ticket
schedule
entrance
local food
souvenirs
historical building
Places in a City
square
church
castle
bridge
beach
market
old town
tourist office

odeComponent.htmlView:
Present Simple
This museum opens at 10 a.m.
Many tourists visit this place every year.

Past Simple
The castle was built in the 15th century.
People lived here many years ago.

Modal Verbs
You can visit the museum.
You should try the local food.

There is / There are
There is a famous monument near the square.

Comparatives & Superlatives
This is the oldest building in the city.

Question Forms
Where is the tourist office?
How much does the ticket cost?
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"20260509083147FVWVYD","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Present Simple
This museum opens at 10 a.m.
Many tourists visit this place every year.

Past Simple
The castle was built in the 15th century.
People lived here many years ago.

Modal Verbs
You can visit the museum.
You should try the local food.

There is / There are
There is a famous monument near the square.

Comparatives & Superlatives
This is the oldest building in the city.

Question Forms
Where is the tourist office?
How much does the ticket cost?

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
• Understanding questions
  • Asking for repetition
  • Taking turns
  • Active listening
  • Using polite language
  • Reacting naturally in conversations

{"ideviceId":"20260509083315FSIBJW","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"
